items.headline: Haaland stuns Brazil — Norway knock out the five-time champs 2-1
items.body: The biggest shock of the World Cup so far. Erling Haaland dragged Norway into the quarterfinals for the first time ever, thumping home a header in the 79th minute and then absolutely leathering a long-range rocket in the 90th to sink Brazil 2-1 outside New York. Neymar pulled one back from the penalty spot deep in stoppage time, but it was too late — it's Brazil's earliest World Cup exit since 1990. Haaland's now up to seven goals, level with Messi and Mbappé for the Golden Boot, and Norway get England next.
items.say: Did you see Haaland just knocked Brazil out of the World Cup? Scored twice, including this absolute thunderbolt from range. Brazil's earliest exit since 1990 — nobody saw that coming.

links.text: ESPN: Haaland sends Brazil to earliest exit since 1990

items.headline: Instant classic at the Azteca — 10-man England survive Mexico 3-2
items.body: What a game. Jude Bellingham scored twice in the space of 98 seconds to stun the home crowd, then Harry Kane buried a penalty for his sixth of the tournament. England had to do it the hard way after Jarell Quansah was sent off, hanging on through nearly half an hour of Mexican pressure to win 3-2 — and handing El Tri their first-ever World Cup loss at the legendary Estadio Azteca. The Three Lions march on to a quarterfinal date with Norway.
items.say: England and Mexico put on maybe the game of the tournament — 3-2 at the Azteca, England down to ten men and hanging on for dear life. Bellingham scored two goals in like 90 seconds. Wild one.

links.text: NBC Sports: Bellingham, Kane sink Mexico at the Azteca

items.headline: Iberian showdown: Yamal's Spain vs. Ronaldo's Portugal today in Dallas
items.body: The pick of Monday's slate is a neighbors' derby with everything. Spain — powered by 18-year-old wonderkid Lamine Yamal — take on Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal in the Round of 16 at Dallas (3pm ET), a first knockout meeting between the two Iberian giants. It's the wonderkid vs. the 41-year-old legend, likely in his last World Cup. Win and you're into the quarters; lose and the summer's over.
items.say: Spain play Portugal this afternoon at the World Cup — basically teenage superstar Yamal against 41-year-old Ronaldo. Neighbors, knockout football, huge stakes. You catching it?

links.text: Al Jazeera: Round of 16 bracket & Portugal-Spain preview

items.headline: USMNT chase history tonight vs. Belgium — a first quarterfinal since 2002
items.body: This is the one the U.S. has been building toward. Pochettino's side take on Belgium in Seattle tonight (8pm ET) with a spot in the World Cup quarterfinals on the line — a stage they haven't reached since 2002. Good news for the home crowd: leading scorer Folarin Balogun is available again after FIFA rescinded the red-card suspension from the Bosnia game. Win and it's the deepest USMNT run in a generation.
items.say: USA play Belgium tonight in Seattle — win and it's the first World Cup quarterfinal since 2002. Balogun's back too. Feels like a real shot. You watching?

links.text: Yahoo Sports: World Cup Round of 16 live updates

items.headline: Osaka topples world No. 1 Sabalenka to reach her first Wimbledon quarterfinal
items.body: The Naomi Osaka comeback tour just hit another gear. Osaka overpowered top-ranked Aryna Sabalenka 6-2, 7-6(2) on a scorching Centre Court — her first win over a world No. 1 since 2019, and the first Wimbledon quarterfinal of her career. She flattened Sabalenka's game with sheer pace off both wings, and now faces Karolina Muchova for a spot in the semis. The four-time Slam champ suddenly looks like a genuine title threat on grass.
items.say: Naomi Osaka just beat the world No. 1 to make her first-ever Wimbledon quarterfinal — first time she's beaten a top-ranked player since 2019. Her comeback's the real deal now.

links.text: WTA: Osaka beats Sabalenka, reaches first Wimbledon quarterfinal

items.headline: Pogačar strikes back — stage 3 win in the Pyrenees puts him back in yellow
items.body: The heavyweight fight is on. Tadej Pogačar powered clear on the summit finish at Les Angles to win stage 3 of the Tour de France, edging arch-rival Jonas Vingegaard by two seconds and grabbing back the yellow jersey (the pair are now dead level on time). Teammate Isaac del Toro delivered the perfect lead-out inside the final kilometer before Pogačar launched a sprint nobody could answer. Three weeks to Paris, and it's already a proper duel.
items.say: Pogačar won the first mountain stage of the Tour and took the yellow jersey back — he and Vingegaard are level on time now. Shaping up to be a real two-man battle. Nice background watching.

links.text: Cyclingnews: 2026 Tour de France live & results

items.headline: Disney's live-action 'Moana' sails into theaters this Friday
items.body: The next big Disney remake is nearly here. The live-action 'Moana' opens July 10, with newcomer Catherine Laga'aia as the wayfinding heroine and Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson back as the demigod Maui — the role he voiced in the beloved 2016 original. Lin-Manuel Miranda's songs get the flesh-and-blood treatment, and it's shaping up as the family go-to for the coming weekend. Cue 'You're Welcome' on repeat.
items.say: The live-action Moana comes out Friday — The Rock's back as Maui. If you've got kids, that's your weekend movie sorted. You a fan of these Disney remakes or over them?

items.headline: Emmy nominations drop Wednesday — 'Pluribus' and 'The Pitt' lead the pack
items.body: TV's big morning is almost here: the 2026 Emmy nominations are announced Wednesday, July 8. The forecast has two shows out front — Apple TV's genre-bending sci-fi 'Pluribus' (Vince Gilligan and Rhea Seehorn, of 'Better Call Saul' fame) is projected to top everything, with HBO Max's real-time ER drama 'The Pitt,' starring Noah Wyle, right behind. Plenty of office debate incoming over who got snubbed.
items.say: Emmy nominations come out Wednesday — looks like that new Vince Gilligan show Pluribus and the ER drama The Pitt are the frontrunners. Always fun to argue about the snubs. You watch either?

items.headline: Jack White is back — new album 'Frozen Charlotte' lands Friday
items.body: The 'Seven Nation Army' man has a new one on the way. Jack White surprise-announced his seventh solo album, 'Frozen Charlotte,' out July 10 on Third Man Records, and the lead single 'Dollar Bill' is already streaming — a raw, blues-rock ripper of slide guitar and crashing drums that critics are calling classic White. A perfect crank-it-up track for the garage or the drive home.
items.say: Jack White — the White Stripes guy — just dropped a new single and has an album out Friday. It's a proper blues-rock stomper. Give 'Dollar Bill' a spin.

links.text: Stereogum: hear Jack White's new single 'Dollar Bill'
